service: Reduce homelessness among children and adults.
oneLiner: HRA still runs homelessness-prevention programs even though the Department of Homeless Services split off as its own agency in 1993 — both trace back to HRA's 1966 founding as the City's unified social-services agency.
summary: HRA was created in 1966 to consolidate New York's welfare and social-services offices into one administration. As homelessness grew into a distinct citywide crisis, the City spun off the Department of Homeless Services as a separate agency in 1993 to focus specifically on shelter operations, but HRA retained programs aimed at preventing homelessness before it happens — rental assistance, eviction prevention, and other supports for families at risk of losing housing.

sources.label: New York City Human Resources Administration — Wikipedia
